classObjective: Solve absolute value equations of the form |expression| = constant and recognize when no solution exists.
workedExamples: 2-5, Example 1 — Solve and Graph Simple Absolute Value Equations
2-5, Example 2 — Identify Absolute Value Equations with No Solution
2-5, Example 3 — Apply Absolute Value to Real-World Problems (Tolerance and Margin of Error)
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: two-case method for |expression| = constant; no-solution recognition when constant is negative
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice solving absolute value equations using the two-case method
independentPractice: Practice problems solving absolute value equations and applying to tolerance/margin-of-error contexts
capReflection: Why does |expression| = negative always have no solution?
